title: Iraq taps Krikor Der-Hagopian as first Armenian Christian ambassador to US
gist: Iraq's pick of US-educated Armenian Christian adviser Krikor Der-Hagopian as ambassador to Washington signals a 'business first' reset of US-Iraq ties, anchored in multi-billion-dollar energy deals (Chevron, Excelerate Energy, Kirkuk-Baniyas pipeline) and Starlink licensing, even as Iran-backed militias and corruption remain structural drags.

insights.claim: Iraq's nomination of an Armenian Christian, US-educated envoy is being read as a deliberate move to recalibrate Washington ties away from a purely security focus toward a 'business first' agenda.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: A diplomatic pivot to commerce signals a more open Iraqi market for US firms — watch for accelerated deal flow in energy and telecom.
insights.quote: refocus the United States' fraught relationship with Iraq away from a purely security focus toward what is being billed as a "business first" agenda
insights.locator: He has been excellent in bilateral relations

insights.claim: If confirmed, Der-Hagopian would become Iraq's first non-Shiite ambassador to Washington in decades, a symbolic break from Iran-aligned political dominance.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: Signals Baghdad is courting US confidence by distancing optics from the Shiite/Iran bloc — a marginal positive for US-Iraq commercial normalization.
insights.quote: the Armenian Christian would become Iraq's first non-Shiite ambassador to Washington in decades

insights.claim: US-Iraq energy talks include multi-billion-dollar negotiations with Chevron to develop southern Iraqi oil fields, floating LNG terminals with Texas-based Excelerate Energy, and rehabilitation of the Kirkuk-to-Baniyas (Syria) oil export pipeline.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: Concrete named deals (CVX, Excelerate/EE) give direct equity exposure to an Iraq energy-opening thesis; the Kirkuk-Baniyas line would re-route Iraqi crude through Syria.
insights.quote: multi-billion dollar negotiations with US major Chevron to develop oil in fields in southern Iraq
insights.locator: These projects include multi-billion dollar negotiations

insights.claim: Elon Musk's Starlink is in line for licensing in Iraq for high-speed internet connectivity as part of the US commercial agenda pushed by Trump's Iraq envoy Tom Barrack.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: Starlink market entry into Iraq adds an emerging-market connectivity datapoint for SpaceX's commercial expansion in MENA.
insights.quote: the licensing of Elon Musk's Starlink for high-speed internet connectivity
insights.locator: These projects include multi-billion dollar negotiations

themes.theme: Structural risks: militias, corruption, US drawdown

insights.claim: Iran is widely seen as having emerged emboldened from its war with Israel and the US, and experts expect it to double down on its alliance with Iraqi Shiite militias that targeted US bases and oil fields.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: Emboldened Iran-backed militias keep operational risk elevated for US-operated oil fields and bases in Iraq, a discount on any energy-deal thesis.
insights.quote: Iran is widely seen as having emerged emboldened from its war with Israel and the United States.
insights.locator: This was an overt reference to Iran-backed Shiite militias

insights.claim: Iraq's government set a September deadline for the anti-ISIS militias to disarm, while past US pressure to dismantle them and cut their revenue has had 'little effect'.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: September is a concrete catalyst date; failure to disarm would reinforce the 'Iran controls Iraq' narrative in Congress and chill US investment.
insights.quote: The Iraqi government has set a September deadline for the militias originally formed to fight ISIS to disarm.
insights.locator: Successive US administrations have pressed Baghdad

insights.claim: Massive corruption and a persistent 'Iran fully controls Iraq' narrative in Congress remain key obstacles, summarized by a source as 'an excellent salesman but stuck with a damaged product'.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: Even with a strong envoy, the US political ceiling on Iraq engagement is low — tempers expectations for how fast commercial ties can deepen.
insights.quote: Der-Hagopian is an excellent salesman but he is stuck with a damaged product
insights.locator: The narrative that Iran fully controls Iraq persists in Congress

insights.claim: The US is winding down Operation Inherent Resolve and shifting from coalition mentorship to a bilateral military format, with most US troops already pulled out by early 2026 and Pentagon funding for Iraqi forces dipping.
insights.speaker: Amberin Zaman
insights.so_what: A reduced US security footprint reshapes the risk environment for US commercial assets in Iraq and the broader regional balance with Iran.
insights.quote: as it pivots away from its mentorship role as the leader of the global coalition to fight the Islamic State known as Operation Inherent Resolve towards a bilateral format
insights.locator: The Pentagon's funding for Iraqi forces has also dipped
